title: Nitinol Shape Memory Alloy Transformation Temperature and Actuation Hysteresis
content: Ni‚āÖ‚āÄTi‚āÖ‚āÄ Nitinol exhibits martensite‚Üíaustenite transformation on heating (Austenite start As = 70 ¬įC, finish Af = 90 ¬įC) and austenite‚Üímartensite on cooling (Martensite start Ms = 60 ¬įC, Mf = 40 ¬įC). Recovery strain 4‚Äď8%; peak recovery stress 600 MPa. Electrical resistance drops 15% through transformation. Joule-heating via PWM current is the standard actuation method. Cycling above 150 ¬įC degrades transformation temperature stability. Hysteresis compensation requires asymmetric heating/cooling PID gains.
